description: State is corrupted after a power-down because registers that needed to survive were not mapped to retention cells.
body: Failure mode: a domain is power-gated but its essential state sits in ordinary registers on the gated supply, so the values are lost when the supply turns off. Detection signals: correct operation until the first power-down, garbage state after wake, and a power-intent file lacking retention on those registers. Do not power-gate the domain while persistent state lacks retention. Safer fallback: map the must-survive registers to retention cells, declare the retention strategy in the power-intent file, and re-verify the save and restore cycle.
